beats.speakable_text: If an unopened banking app is familiar, this money avoidance pattern needs a better reset.
Trying to outrun money usually makes the loop more expensive. More avoidance, more emotional spending. And less clarity about what you actually value.

Money avoidance often persists because financial decisions get made by the threat system instead of by your actual values. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
Try this. Open one account, write the one number that matters this week. And decide one small next step. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. A budget is a values statement, not a punishment.
